other_observers: Maggie Boswell with me.  Met Duane Nelson and John Drummond who had heard the birds earlier.
specific_location: Alfred M. Bailey Bird Nesting Area
description: Maggie Boswell and I met Duane Nelson and John Drummond, and Duane took us to the spot where they had heard and seen Cassia Crossbills.  Duane left after about 30 minutes during which time we had heard and seen many (mainly type 2) Red Crossbills.  About 10 minutes later, Maggie recorded on the Merlin app a group of crossbills that sounded quite different (sharper and faster calls) that Merlin identified as Cassia.  This recording is attached to this report.  I saw the birds making the sounds as they flew in, and it looked like a group of 4 birds and I definitely saw a female and a juvenile bird through my binoculars.  At that distance, I couldn't see any features on the birds that would distinguish them from Red Crossbills.
